full_prompt: Design a 17 mm projectile launcher assembly for a RoboMaster RMUC 2025 Standard Robot. The launcher uses two counter-rotating flywheels to fire 17 mm polycarbonate BBs (nominal projectile diameter 16.8 +/- 0.2 mm, nominal mass 3.2 g) at a muzzle velocity limited by RMUC 2025 Rules v1.1.0 to 30 m/s for the Standard class with a heat cap (barrel heat model accumulates per shot and dissipates per cooling rate). The launcher is one sub-assembly within the robot's 17 mm turret; total chassis mass budget for the Standard class is 20 kg maximum. The launcher assembly envelope must fit within 150 mm (length, along bore axis) x 80 mm (width) x 80 mm (height). Components: (a) a single-body machined aluminium launcher housing that provides two bearing bores for flywheel shafts spaced 32 mm apart center-to-center, a 17.5 mm inner diameter smooth-bore barrel of length 80 mm, and a 4-bolt interface (M4, 40 mm square pattern) to the turret yoke; (b) two flywheel rotors of 60 mm outer diameter, 15 mm wide, machined from 2024-T3 aluminium, each driven by a 3508 brushless motor through a direct-coupled shaft; (c) a feed throat that constrains the projectile to a single file queue. Material for the housing is 6061-T6 aluminium with Young's modulus 69 GPa, Poisson's ratio 0.33, density 2700 kg/m^3, yield strength 276 MPa, ultimate tensile strength 310 MPa. Flywheel material is 2024-T3 aluminium (73.1 GPa, 0.33, 2780 kg/m^3, 345 MPa yield, 483 MPa UTS). Load cases: LC1 (Shot reaction) — an impulsive 1 kN bore-axis reaction force on the barrel root from a BB transferring 30 m/s muzzle velocity over a 5 ms contact, with turret interface fully fixed; LC2 (Flywheel spin) — 10,000 rpm steady rotation of each 60 mm flywheel, giving centrifugal load at rim of 2.8 kN equivalent; LC3 (Barrel heat soak) — uniform thermal rise of 40 K across the barrel, representative of sustained 10 Hz fire with rule-limited heat dissipation, while housing bolt pattern is fixed; LC4 (Turret crash) — 20 g lateral shock on the launcher assembly with turret bolt pattern fully fixed (robot tipover). Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises in housing under LC1 must stay below 184 MPa (6061-T6 yield/1.5); (R2) max von Mises in flywheel under LC2 must stay below 230 MPa (2024-T3 yield/1.5); (R3) max radial growth of the flywheel under LC2 must stay below 0.15 mm so bearing preload is preserved; (R4) combined LC1 + LC3 thermal-structural analysis peak stress must stay below 200 MPa in the housing; (R5) under LC4, peak von Mises in housing must stay below 184 MPa; (R6) total launcher-assembly mass must be less than 600 g so the 20 kg chassis budget is respected; (R7) barrel inner diameter under LC3 must not drop below 17.0 mm or grow above 18.0 mm (to keep muzzle velocity within RMUC's +/- 2 m/s chrono tolerance). Primary verification: linear static structural FEA for LC1, LC2, LC4; thermal-structural coupled linear FEA for LC3 (and combined LC1 + LC3); linear rotordynamic centrifugal loading for LC2. Vibration modal analysis, CFD, and radiation are explicitly not required. Output one STEP AP242 assembly and a report listing total mass, max von Mises per case, barrel ID change under LC3, and flywheel radial growth under LC2.
short_prompt:
Design the 17 mm projectile launcher assembly for a RoboMaster RMUC 2025 Standard Robot turret. Muzzle velocity capped at 30 m/s per RMUC 2025 Rules v1.1.0; Standard-class chassis mass cap 20 kg.

Envelope: assembly 150 x 80 x 80 mm; 6061-T6 housing with two flywheel-shaft bearing bores on 32 mm centers and an 80 mm smooth 17.5 mm ID barrel; two 2024-T3 aluminum flywheels 60 mm OD x 15 mm driven by 3508 motors; 4 x M4 turret-yoke bolts on a 40 mm square pattern.

Factor regime: yield FoS 1.5 on housing and flywheel for the primary cases; combined LC1+LC3 peak <= 200 MPa in housing; barrel ID must stay within 17.0-18.0 mm under thermal.

Load cases.
- Shot reaction: impulsive 1 kN along the bore axis over 5 ms at the barrel root.
- Flywheel spin: 10,000 rpm steady on each 60 mm flywheel (~2.8 kN rim centrifugal).
- Barrel heat soak: uniform 40 K rise across the barrel (sustained 10 Hz fire).
- Turret crash: 20 g lateral shock with turret bolts fixed.

prompt.title: RoboMaster RMUC 2025 17 mm launcher assembly
prompt.brief: Flywheel-based 17 mm BB launcher sized to the RMUC Standard class 30 m/s muzzle cap and 20 kg chassis budget, with barrel-heat thermal load and shot-reaction impulse included.

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Modal not gated by RMUC rules.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Bore aerodynamics modeled by chrono test, not CFD.

eval_coverage: tight bore tolerance preservation under heat
